title: Comprehensive Audit Trail for Successful CDE Administrator Logons

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 10.2.2 ā€” Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI-DSS v3.2.1 10.1 Audit trail linking access to user ā€” UC builds the comprehensive audit trail for CDE administrator logons, linking every access to a specific user identity.

app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833)
spl:
(index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 earliest=-24h)
 OR (index=linux sourcetype="linux:audit" type="USER_LOGIN" earliest=-24h)
| lookup pci_cde_servers.csv coalesce(ComputerName,host) as host OUTPUT in_cde
| where in_cde="true"
| eval user_id=coalesce(TargetUserName,acct)
| eval src=coalesce(IpAddress,addr)
| table _time, host, user_id, LogonType, src, TerminalSessionId
| sort - _time
description: Produces a consolidated view of successful interactive and remote logons to CDE admin jump accounts including user ID, time, origin, and object accessed for all individual user access to cardholder systems.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS, PCI-DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes linux, windows and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security, linux:audit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Ensure 4624 includes workstation name and IP fields populated. (2) Mask sensitive columns in shared dashboards. (3) Long retention index or archive for 12+ months. (4) Map to quarterly access review samples. Indexes required: `index=linux`, `index=windows`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`, `sourcetype=linux:audit`; Lookups: `pci_cde_servers`, `pci_cde_servers.csv`.
visualization: Table (sortable audit view), timeline, pivot by user_id.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux

knownFalsePositives: CDE access from authorized payment ops, scheduled batch reconciliation, or PCI assessor activities.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ā€¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833).
ā€¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=windows` EventCode 4624; `index=linux` `auditd` type=USER_LOGIN.
ā€¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ā€” Configure data collection
(1) Ensure 4624 includes workstation name and IP fields populated. (2) Mask sensitive columns in shared dashboards. (3) Long retention index or archive for 12+ months. (4) Map to quarterly access review samples.

Step 2 ā€” Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
(index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 earliest=-24h)
 OR (index=linux sourcetype="linux:audit" type="USER_LOGIN" earliest=-24h)
| lookup pci_cde_servers.csv coalesce(ComputerName,host) as host OUTPUT in_cde
| where in_cde="true"
| eval user_id=coalesce(TargetUserName,acct)
| eval src=coalesce(IpAddress,addr)
| table _time, host, user_id, LogonType, src, TerminalSessionId
| sort - _time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Comprehensive Audit Trail for Successful CDE Administrator Logons (PCI DSS Req 10.2.1, 10.2.2)** ā€” Produces a consolidated view of successful interactive and remote logons to CDE admin jump accounts including user ID, time, origin, and object accessed for all individual user access to cardholder systems.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=windows` EventCode 4624; `index=linux` `auditd` type=USER_LOGIN. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedā€”rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows, linux; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security, linux:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ā€¢ Scopes the data: index=windows, index=linux, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
ā€¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
ā€¢ Filters the current rows with `where in_cde="true"` ā€” typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
ā€¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **user_id** ā€” applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
ā€¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **src** ā€” applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

ā€¢ Orders rows with `sort` ā€” combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Comprehensive Audit Trail for Successful CDE Administrator Logons (PCI DSS Req 10.2.1, 10.2.2)** ā€” Produces a consolidated view of successful interactive and remote logons to CDE admin jump accounts including user ID, time, origin, and object accessed for all individual user access to cardholder systems.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=windows` EventCode 4624; `index=linux` `auditd` type=USER_LOGIN. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedā€”rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ā€¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` ā€” enable acceleration for that model.
ā€¢ Orders rows with `sort` ā€” combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: _time, LogonType, TerminalSessionId, host, src, user_id.

Step 3 ā€” Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 ā€” Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (sortable audit view), timeline, pivot by user_id.
grandmaExplanation: We produce a consolidated view of successful interactive and remote logons to CDE admin jump accounts including user ID, time, origin, and object accessed for all individual user access to cardholder systems. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Authentication (CIM data model).
